notes: Preservation fixed-method catalog for SSA Layer Spine (L2 method maps).
Methods are templates: they encode steps, cues, parameters, and safety checks. User/household overrides should be applied via layer_overrides.
This catalog includes home and small-scale commercial variants; methods marked commercial_only should be gated in UI unless enabled.

safety.pressureCanningBotulismNote: Low-acid foods require pressure canning to control botulism risk.
safety.cleanHandsNote: Wash hands; sanitize surfaces; use clean tools to avoid contamination.

cueLexicon.active_bubbles: Visible bubbling indicates fermentation activity (CO₂ production).
cueLexicon.pellicle: A surface film can occur in some ferments (e.g., vinegar/kombucha); evaluate smell and appearance.
cueLexicon.krausen: Foamy cap in active beverage fermentation; normal during peak activity.
cueLexicon.jar_seal: A sealed jar lid is concave and does not flex/pop when pressed in the center.
cueLexicon.vacuum_tight: A firm, tight bag with no trapped air pockets around the food indicates a good vacuum seal.
cueLexicon.case_harden: Dry outer layer with moist interior (too-fast drying). Reduce heat, increase airflow, or slice thinner.
cueLexicon.freezer_burn: Dry, pale patches from dehydration in freezer; reduce air exposure with better wrapping.
cueLexicon.salt_equilibrium: Equilibrium curing uses precise salt % by weight; time is for texture/flavor, not salt penetration.

parameters.label: Jar Size
parameters.helpText: Processing time varies by jar size and recipe.

parameters.label: Altitude (ft)
parameters.helpText: Adjust processing time for altitude per tested guidance.

parameters.label: Target pH
parameters.helpText: Must be < 4.6 for safe water-bath canning.

tips: Use tested recipes; verify acidification for tomatoes.
If pH is uncertain or >4.6, switch to pressure canning.
Hot soapy wash; keep jars warm to prevent thermal shock.

visual: Water at full rolling boil before timing.
tips: Jars must be covered by 1–2 inches of water.

defaultSteps.title: Fill jars; remove bubbles; apply lids

tips: Wipe rims; keep headspace consistent.

defaultSteps.title: Process at boil for set time

tips: Start timer when boil resumes.
Rest 12–24h before moving; refrigerate unsealed jars.
Remove rings for storage; check periodically for leaks.

notes: If seal fails, refrigerate and use soon, or reprocess within 24 hours with new lid.

parameters.label: Venting Time (minutes)
parameters.helpText: Vent steam to purge air before pressurizing.

defaultSteps.title: Select tested recipe; confirm low-acid pathway

tips: Use tested pressure-canning times for the food type and jar size.

defaultSteps.title: Wash jars; prep lids; keep hot

tips: Warm jars prevent cracking during processing.

defaultSteps.title: Load canner with rack + water; fill jars

tips: Follow canner manual for water level (often 2–3 inches).
Start vent timer after steady steam begins.
Maintain pressure throughout; if it drops, restart timing per guidance.
Do not force cool; wait for pressure to reach zero.
Label and store; refrigerate unsealed jars.

notes: Low-acid foods require pressure canning to manage botulism risk.

parameters.label: Processing Time (minutes)
parameters.helpText: Steam canning is typically limited to recipes with <=45 min processing.

tips: Use tested guidance for steam canning; respect time limits.
Follow manufacturer water level and venting steps.

defaultSteps.title: Process at steady steam per time

tips: Start timing once steady steam is achieved.
Cool 12–24h before moving.
Uniform slices dry evenly; blot high-moisture foods.

defaultSteps.title: Set temp; load trays with airflow gaps

tips: Avoid overlap; leave space for airflow.
Check early to prevent over-drying.

checks.label: No case hardening (outside dry, inside wet)

tips: Condition dried fruit: jar 7–10 days, shake daily; watch for condensation.
Use oxygen absorbers for long storage; label clearly.
Check periodically for moisture/mold.

parameters.label: Climate Suitability
parameters.helpText: Humid climates increase spoilage risk; consider dehydrator instead.

defaultSteps.title: Slice thin; pretreat if needed (lemon, blanch)

tips: Thin, uniform slices reduce spoilage window.

defaultSteps.title: Set racks in sun with airflow; cover with screen

tips: Elevate racks; keep away from dust and animals.
Night humidity can re-wet food; bring indoors each evening.
If any softness remains, dry longer before storage.
Pre-freezing speeds cycles and improves texture.
Check dryness; add extra dry time if centers are cool/soft.
Minimize exposure to humidity; seal promptly.
Rotate stock; label with batch + best-by.

outputs.shelfLife: 10–25 years (if sealed in mylar with O₂ absorbers; quality varies)

tips: Cool hot food before freezing to protect freezer and food quality.
Double-wrap meats; press out air to reduce freezer burn.
Spread packages; once solid, stack to save space.
Use FIFO; label with date and contents.
Uniform pieces blanch evenly.

audio: Boil returns quickly after adding veg.
tips: Start timer when water returns to boil.

defaultSteps.title: Shock in ice water; drain well

tips: Drain thoroughly to prevent ice crystals.
Pre-freeze on tray for loose pieces.

defaultSteps.title: Weigh veg; calculate salt; massage to brine

tips: Salt draws water; you want veg submerged in brine.
Keep everything below brine to prevent mold.
Kahm yeast is common; mold fuzzy/colored = remove/assess.

defaultSteps.title: Taste for sourness; move to cold storage

tips: When flavor is right, refrigerate to slow fermentation.
Keep covered; use clean utensils to avoid contamination.

defaultSteps.title: Make brine; pack cucumbers with spices

tips: Trim blossom end to reduce softening.
Keep under brine; top off with brine if needed.

defaultSteps.title: Taste; cold-store to stop at desired sourness

tips: Move to refrigerator for long-term storage.

parameters.label: Starting Alcohol %
parameters.helpText: Acetobacter converts alcohol to acetic acid; too high ABV can stall.

tips: No sulfites if possible; they inhibit bacteria.
Oxygen is required; do not seal airtight.
If mold develops (fuzzy/colored), discard.
Pasteurize (gentle heat) to stabilize; or keep raw for live culture.
Cool before adding SCOBY to avoid heat damage.
Keep out of direct sun; allow airflow.
Burp bottles to avoid overpressure.
Cold slows fermentation and carbonation.

parameters.label: Vinegar:Water Ratio
parameters.helpText: Higher vinegar increases acidity and tang.

defaultSteps.title: Slice veg; pack jar with spices

tips: Thin slices pickle faster.

Flavor improves after 24–72 hours.

defaultSteps.title: Add acid to each jar per guidance

defaultSteps.title: Proceed with tested tomato canning recipe

parameters.label: Curing Salt #1
parameters.helpText: Use per recipe for safety/flavor in cured meats; follow legal guidance.

tips: Precision matters; write calculations on label.
Flip daily or every other day for even cure.

defaultSteps.title: Cure until firm; rinse/soak if needed

defaultSteps.title: Dry surface; smoke/cook or age per method

tips: Proceed to smoking, cooking, or drying steps as appropriate.

Cool quickly; store safely.

methods.title: Cold Smoking (Flavor Only; Requires Prior Cure/Control)

defaultSteps.title: Confirm food is cured/safe for cold smoke

tips: Cold smoke does not cook; use only on properly cured items or foods safe to cold smoke.

tips: Keep chamber cool; avoid warm-zone bacterial growth.
Rest refrigerated; smoke flavor evens out.

notes: Cold smoking can be risky without temperature/humidity control.

parameters.label: Salt Ratio
parameters.helpText: Traditional heavy salting aims for water activity reduction.

tips: Remove bloodline; keep cold while working.
Maintain refrigeration for safety unless following validated traditional method for your climate.
Firm texture indicates moisture loss.
Soak before use to remove excess salt.

outputs.shelfLife: weeks to months depending on salt/dry level
